key_insight: The paper reframes structured preconditioning as a geometric distance-and-reachability problem: how far a restricted metric family is from achieving a target condition-number class.
engineering_relevance: It gives practical certificate tests for deciding whether diagonal, block, or Kronecker-structured preconditioners can meet conditioning goals before expensive solver tuning.
method: Defines restricted dynamic geometric complexity as an intrinsic certificate distance under metric-family constraints.
Proves monotonicity and submanifold-distance principles, plus LMI feasibility formulations for diagonal and block reachability.
Derives exact or computable certificates for special cases, including 2D diagonal complexity, Kronecker projection/normal-equation tests, and Hessian-relative Loewner-sandwich conditions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports deterministic toy and synthetic workflows that verify diagonal expression gaps, block primal/dual certificates, Kronecker spectral width, and Hessian-relative candidate certificates on small quadratic instances.
watch_item: Several results are presented as diagnostic or certificate tools rather than full optimizer characterizations, so scalability and practical solver overhead may limit direct deployment on large problems.
paper_title: Restricted Dynamic Geometric Complexity: Certificates for Structured Preconditioning
